frame_label: The Makeup Paradox: Unnatural Yet Expected
generated_comment: Honestly I prefer girls who keep it simple and natural. In photos full makeup, filters, everything will be there, then in real life totally different means what is the need for all that? At the same time basic grooming is important also, otherwise it looks like they don't care about themselves.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it places women in a double bind: criticizing visible makeup as deceptive while still expecting them to maintain an acceptable level of beauty work. It encodes the 'natural beauty' preference, deception/catfishing framing, and appearance obligation surface manifestations.
original_frame: Other Man's Ownership Over Woman's Own Consent.
persona_text: You are Harish. Age 31. Single. A man in Chennai. You are posting anonymously on a Tamil Facebook group after a video went viral of a young woman being stopped by her father and brothers at a bus stand and forcibly taken home because she was trying to leave with her boyfriend. Most comments are supporting the father. You are adding your comment and explaining why you think the father was right and what would have happened if he had not intervened and what daughters owe their families.
